findings.message: The create_item() function can still be called directly, bypassing the normalization enforced by create_item_normalized(). While a docstring warning exists, Python does not prevent internal code paths or tests from using the raw function, potentially storing un-normalized names and breaking consistency guarantees.
findings.suggestion: Consider raising an exception in create_item() or marking it with @deprecated decorator to enforce the use of create_item_normalized(). Alternatively, refactor to make name normalization mandatory at the model layer or add a pre-commit hook to detect direct create_item() calls.
